extracted_rules.title: Migration Files Must Follow Strict Naming Convention
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure database migration files follow the required timestamp-based naming pattern to maintain chronological order and enable proper migration tracking
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Migration files in core/server/data/migrations/versions/ (excluding versions 1.x, 2.x, 3.x) match the pattern: YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-descriptive-name-with-hyphens (e.g., 2022-04-05-12-00-add-member-alert-setting.js)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Migration files do not follow the required timestamp format or use underscores, camelCase, or other naming styles

extracted_rules.title: Enforce Module Boundary Between core/shared and core/server
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain architectural separation by preventing core/shared modules from depending on core/server code, ensuring shared code remains truly reusable across server and frontend
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Files in core/shared/ do not contain require() or import statements that reference core/server/ paths
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Files in core/shared/ import or require modules from core/server/

extracted_rules.title: Enforce Module Boundary Between core/shared and core/frontend
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain architectural separation by preventing core/shared modules from depending on core/frontend code, ensuring shared code remains truly reusable across server and frontend
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Files in core/shared/ do not contain require() or import statements that reference core/frontend/ paths
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Files in core/shared/ import or require modules from core/frontend/

extracted_rules.title: Database Schema Must Not Include User Audit Fields
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent using created_by and updated_by fields in database schema to enforce the use of the action log for tracking user actions, providing better audit trails and separation of concerns
extracted_rules.success_criteria: The schema definition in core/server/data/schema/schema.js does not contain properties named 'created_by' or 'updated_by'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: The schema definition includes 'created_by' or 'updated_by' fields

extracted_rules.title: Database Migrations Must Not Use Loop Constructs
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent performance issues in database migrations by prohibiting loop constructs (for, while, forEach) that can cause migrations to run slowly or fail on large datasets
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Migration files in core/server/data/migrations/versions/ do not contain for statements, for-of statements, for-in statements, while statements, forEach calls, or _.each calls
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Migration files contain any loop constructs including for, for-of, for-in, while, forEach, or _.each

extracted_rules.title: Database Migrations Must Not Use Multiple JOIN Statements
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent complex and poorly performing database queries in migrations by prohibiting multiple JOIN operations in a single Knex query block
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Migration files do not chain multiple join/innerJoin/leftJoin calls on a single Knex query object
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Migration files contain Knex queries with multiple chained join operations

extracted_rules.title: Database Migrations Must Not Return Early from Loops
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent data processing errors in migrations by ensuring all records are processed when loops are used (in legacy migrations or utility functions)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Migration files do not contain return statements inside loop bodies
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Migration files contain return statements within loop constructs

extracted_rules.title: API Endpoint Controllers Must Have Type Annotations
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure type safety and IDE support for API endpoint controllers by requiring JSDoc type annotations from @tryghost/api-framework
extracted_rules.success_criteria: API endpoint controller objects in core/server/api/endpoints/ include JSDoc type comment '@type {import('@tryghost/api-framework').Controller}'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: API endpoint controllers lack the required JSDoc type annotation

extracted_rules.title: API Endpoints Must Enforce Complexity Limits
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain code quality and prevent overly complex API endpoint implementations by enforcing complexity metrics on endpoint handler functions
extracted_rules.success_criteria: API endpoint files in core/server/api/endpoints/ pass the 'ghost/ghost-custom/max-api-complexity' ESLint rule
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: API endpoint functions exceed the maximum allowed cyclomatic complexity threshold

extracted_rules.title: Internationalization Files Must Use Kebab-Case Naming
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent file naming across all i18n translation files to maintain a uniform codebase structure and simplify automated tooling
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All files in ghost/i18n/ use kebab-case naming (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots only): matching pattern ^[a-z0-9.-]+$
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: i18n files use camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, or contain uppercase letters

extracted_rules.title: Admin React Apps Must Use Kebab-Case File Naming
extracted_rules.objective: Enforce consistent kebab-case file naming convention across React admin applications to align with the codebase migration to kebab-case naming standards
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All files in apps/admin-x-* directories use kebab-case naming (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots only): matching pattern ^[a-z0-9.-]+$
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Admin app files use camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, or contain uppercase letters

extracted_rules.title: React Components Must Sort JSX Props in Standard Order
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent and readable JSX code by enforcing a standard ordering of component props: reserved props first, callbacks last, shorthand props last
extracted_rules.success_criteria: JSX elements in React files have props ordered according to: reserved props (key, ref) first, then regular props alphabetically, then callbacks, then shorthand props last
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: JSX elements have props in non-standard order (e.g., callbacks before regular props, shorthand before callbacks)

extracted_rules.title: React Button Elements Must Have Explicit Type Attribute
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent unintended form submissions and unclear button semantics by requiring all button elements to explicitly specify their type attribute (button, submit, or reset)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All <button> elements in React files include an explicit type attribute
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Button elements lack a type attribute or rely on browser default behavior

extracted_rules.title: React List Rendering Must Not Use Array Index as Key
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent rendering bugs and performance issues by prohibiting the use of array indices as React key props, which can cause incorrect component state when lists are reordered or modified
extracted_rules.success_criteria: React components rendering lists use stable, unique identifiers (e.g., item.id) as key props, not array indices
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: List items use array index as the key prop (e.g., key={index} or key={i})

extracted_rules.title: Tailwind CSS Classes Must Follow Standard Ordering
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent and maintainable Tailwind CSS class lists by enforcing the standard ordering convention defined in the Tailwind configuration
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Tailwind utility classes in className attributes follow the ordering specified in tailwind.config.cjs (layout, positioning, sizing, spacing, typography, visual, etc.)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Tailwind classes are in non-standard order or randomly organized

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript Files Must Enable Strict Type Checking
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure type safety and catch potential runtime errors at compile time by requiring strict mode and noImplicitAny in all TypeScript configurations
extracted_rules.success_criteria: tsconfig.json files include 'strict': true and 'noImplicitAny': true in compilerOptions
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: TypeScript configuration files have strict or noImplicitAny set to false or omitted

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Use 4-Space Indentation
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent code formatting across the codebase by enforcing 4-space indentation for all source files (except JSON and YAML which use 2 spaces)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All JavaScript, TypeScript, and other source files use 4 spaces for indentation levels
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Source files use tabs, 2-space indentation, or mixed indentation styles

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Use Single Quotes for Strings
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent string literal formatting across the codebase by requiring single quotes for all string values (template literals are allowed)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: String literals in JavaScript and TypeScript files use single quotes, except when using template literals for interpolation
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: String literals use double quotes without justified reason

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Always Use Semicolons
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent subtle bugs from automatic semicolon insertion and maintain explicit statement termination by requiring semicolons at the end of all statements
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All JavaScript and TypeScript statements end with explicit semicolons
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Statements rely on automatic semicolon insertion (ASI) instead of explicit semicolons

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Use let or const Instead of var
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent hoisting-related bugs and unclear variable scoping by prohibiting var declarations in favor of block-scoped let and const
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All variable declarations use let (for reassignable variables) or const (for constants), never var
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains var declarations

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Use Strict Equality Operators
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent type coercion bugs by requiring strict equality checks (=== and !==) instead of loose equality (== and !=)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All equality comparisons use === or !== operators
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains == or != operators for equality checks

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Not Use console.* Calls
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent debug statements from being committed to production code by prohibiting console.log, console.warn, and other console method calls
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Source files do not contain console.log, console.warn, console.error, or other console method calls
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains calls to console methods

extracted_rules.title: Package Manager Must Be Yarn v1
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent dependency management and workspace functionality by requiring Yarn v1 for all package operations, as the monorepo relies on Yarn workspaces
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All package management commands (install, add, remove, run) use yarn, not npm or other package managers
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code documentation, scripts, or CI configuration references npm or other package managers for dependency management

extracted_rules.title: Node.js Version Must Match Engine Requirement
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure compatibility and prevent runtime errors by enforcing the specific Node.js version required by the Ghost application
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Development and production environments use Node.js version matching the engines.node specification in ghost/core/package.json (^22.13.1)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Environment uses a Node.js version outside the specified semver range

extracted_rules.title: All C# Source Files Must Include MIT License Header
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure legal compliance and proper attribution by requiring the standardized .NET Foundation MIT license header at the beginning of every C# source file
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Every .cs file starts with the exact two-line comment header: '// Licensed to the .NET Foundation under one or more agreements.' followed by '// The .NET Foundation licenses this file to you under the MIT license.'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: C# files are missing the license header, have an incorrect or modified license header, or have the header in the wrong location (not at the start of the file)

extracted_rules.title: Use File-Scoped Namespace Declarations
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistency with modern C# conventions and reduce indentation levels by using file-scoped namespace declarations instead of traditional block-scoped namespaces
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All C# files use file-scoped namespace syntax (namespace declaration ending with semicolon without braces) as in 'namespace Microsoft.AspNetCore.Feature;'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: C# files use traditional namespace declarations with opening and closing braces

extracted_rules.title: Private and Internal Fields Must Use Underscore Prefix with camelCase
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent code style and improve readability by enforcing the underscore prefix naming convention for private and internal instance fields
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All private and internal instance fields are named with an underscore prefix followed by camelCase (e.g., '_antiforgery', '_tokenStore', '_dbOperations')
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Private or internal instance fields lack the underscore prefix, use PascalCase, or use other naming patterns inconsistent with the convention

extracted_rules.title: Use ArgumentNullException Throw Helpers for Parameter Validation
extracted_rules.objective: Standardize null parameter validation and improve code clarity by using modern throw helper methods instead of traditional null checks with manual exception throwing
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Null parameter validation uses ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(parameter) or similar throw helper methods (ThrowIfNullOrEmpty, ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace) at the beginning of public methods
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code uses manual null checks with 'if (parameter == null) throw new ArgumentNullException()' pattern or lacks null parameter validation where appropriate

extracted_rules.title: Test Classes Must Follow Naming Convention
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistency and discoverability of test files by enforcing standardized test class naming patterns that clearly identify their purpose
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test class names end with 'Test' or 'Tests' suffix (e.g., 'DefaultAntiforgeryTest', 'SqlServerCacheTests') and are located in directories named 'test'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test classes lack the 'Test' or 'Tests' suffix, use inconsistent naming patterns, or are not clearly identifiable as test classes

extracted_rules.title: Test Methods Must Use Arrange-Act-Assert Pattern with Comments
extracted_rules.objective: Improve test readability and maintainability by enforcing explicit structural comments that separate the test setup, execution, and verification phases
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test methods include explicit '// Arrange', '// Act', and '// Assert' comments (or combined '// Act & Assert' when appropriate) that clearly delineate the three phases of the test
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test methods lack the AAA pattern comments, use inconsistent comment formatting, or mix phases without clear separation

extracted_rules.title: Public APIs Must Have XML Documentation Comments
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure comprehensive API documentation for consumers by requiring XML documentation on all public types, methods, properties, and events
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All public APIs include XML documentation comments with at minimum <summary> tags, and <param>, <returns>, <exception>, and <remarks> tags where applicable
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Public types, methods, properties, or events lack XML documentation comments, have incomplete documentation, or have missing required tags like <param> for parameters

extracted_rules.title: Async Methods Must Be Named with Async Suffix
extracted_rules.objective: Improve code clarity and follow .NET naming conventions by ensuring all asynchronous methods are clearly identified with the 'Async' suffix
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All methods returning Task or Task<T> are named with the 'Async' suffix (e.g., 'GetAsync', 'ValidateRequestAsync', 'SaveAsync')
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Methods returning Task or Task<T> lack the 'Async' suffix or methods not returning Task inappropriately use the suffix

extracted_rules.title: Async Methods Must Use ConfigureAwait(false) in Library Code
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent deadlocks and improve performance in library code by ensuring async operations do not capture the synchronization context unnecessarily
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All await expressions in library code (non-test, non-sample projects) use .ConfigureAwait(false) when awaiting tasks
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Await expressions in library code omit ConfigureAwait(false) or use ConfigureAwait(true), potentially causing synchronization context capture

extracted_rules.title: Async Methods Must Accept CancellationToken Parameter
extracted_rules.objective: Enable cooperative cancellation and improve responsiveness by ensuring all async methods provide a mechanism for callers to cancel long-running operations
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Public async methods include a CancellationToken parameter, typically with a default value of 'default(CancellationToken)' or 'default', positioned as the last parameter
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Async methods that perform I/O or long-running operations lack a CancellationToken parameter or position it incorrectly in the parameter list

extracted_rules.title: Use Primary Constructor Syntax Where Appropriate
extracted_rules.objective: Reduce boilerplate code and improve readability by using C# 12 primary constructor syntax for simple dependency injection scenarios
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Classes with simple constructor-to-field assignment patterns use primary constructor syntax (parameters in parentheses after class name) instead of traditional constructor with field assignments
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Classes with simple dependency injection patterns use traditional constructors with repetitive parameter-to-field assignments when primary constructor syntax would be more concise

extracted_rules.title: Service Registration Extension Methods Must Return IServiceCollection
extracted_rules.objective: Enable method chaining and follow the fluent API pattern by ensuring all service registration extension methods return the service collection for further configuration
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Extension methods on IServiceCollection that register services return the IServiceCollection instance to enable fluent chaining
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Service registration extension methods return void or other types, breaking the fluent API pattern and preventing method chaining

extracted_rules.title: Use TryAdd Methods for Optional Service Registration
extracted_rules.objective: Allow consumers to override default service registrations by using TryAdd methods that only register services if no implementation already exists
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Service registration for overridable services uses TryAddSingleton, TryAddScoped, TryAddTransient, or TryAddEnumerable instead of direct Add methods
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Service registration uses Add methods (AddSingleton, AddScoped, AddTransient) for services that should be overridable, preventing consumer customization

extracted_rules.title: Sealed Keyword Required for Internal Implementation Classes
extracted_rules.objective: Improve performance by preventing virtual dispatch overhead and clarify design intent by sealing internal classes not intended for inheritance
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Internal classes that are not explicitly designed for inheritance are marked with the 'sealed' keyword (e.g., 'internal sealed class DefaultAntiforgery')
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Internal implementation classes lack the sealed keyword even though they are not designed for inheritance, potentially allowing unintended subclassing

extracted_rules.title: Use Source-Generated Logging with LoggerMessage Attribute
extracted_rules.objective: Improve logging performance and type safety by using compile-time generated logging methods instead of runtime string formatting
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Logging code uses partial methods with [LoggerMessage] attribute that specify log level, event ID, and message template for compile-time generation
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Logging uses traditional ILogger methods (_logger.LogInformation, _logger.LogWarning) with runtime string formatting instead of source-generated logging

extracted_rules.title: Middleware Must Implement Fast-Path Synchronous Invoke Method
extracted_rules.objective: Optimize middleware pipeline performance by providing a synchronous code path for common scenarios that do not require awaiting async operations
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Middleware classes implement a synchronous Invoke method that returns Task and only calls a separate async method (e.g., InvokeAwaited) when async operations are actually needed
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Middleware implements only async Invoke methods or uses async/await unnecessarily in the hot path when synchronous execution would suffice

extracted_rules.title: Use Curly Braces for All Control Flow Statements
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent subtle bugs and improve code consistency by requiring braces around all control flow statement bodies, even single-line statements
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All if, else, for, foreach, while, and do-while statements use curly braces to enclose their bodies, even for single statements
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Control flow statements omit curly braces for single-line bodies, making the code prone to errors when modifications are made

extracted_rules.title: Opening Braces Must Be on New Line (Allman Style)
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain visual consistency and improve code readability by enforcing the Allman brace style where opening braces appear on their own line
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All opening braces for methods, classes, namespaces, control flow statements, and other blocks appear on a new line after the declaration
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Opening braces appear on the same line as the declaration (K&R style) instead of on a new line

extracted_rules.title: Test Projects Must Use xUnit Framework
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistency across the codebase and leverage xUnit's features by standardizing on xUnit as the test framework for all test projects
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test methods use xUnit attributes ([Fact], [Theory], [InlineData]) and assertions (Assert.Equal, Assert.Throws, etc.)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test projects use alternative testing frameworks (MSTest, NUnit) inconsistent with the repository standard

extracted_rules.title: Unit Test Projects Must Follow Directory Structure Convention
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent project organization by placing test projects in 'test' directories parallel to their corresponding 'src' directories
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test projects are located in directories named 'test' that are siblings to 'src' directories containing the code under test
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test projects are placed in inconsistent locations such as within src directories, in misnamed directories, or without clear correspondence to source projects

extracted_rules.title: Repository and Service Classes Must Follow Naming Conventions
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistency and discoverability by requiring repository classes to use 'Prisma<Entity>Repository' pattern and service classes to use '<Entity>Service' pattern, with filenames matching class names exactly in PascalCase
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Repository files are named 'Prisma<Entity>Repository.ts' with matching exported class names (e.g., PrismaAppRepository), and service files are named '<Entity>Service.ts' with matching class names (e.g., MembershipService)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Repository or service files use generic names like 'app.ts', use dot-suffixes like '.service.ts' or '.repository.ts', or have filename/class name mismatches

extracted_rules.title: Prevent Circular Dependencies Between Core Packages
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain clear architectural boundaries and prevent circular dependencies by enforcing import restrictions between core packages (lib, app-store, features, trpc)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: The lib package does not import from app-store, features, or trpc; app-store does not import from features or trpc; features does not import from trpc; and trpc does not import from apps/web
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains imports that violate the dependency hierarchy, such as lib importing from features, app-store importing from trpc, or any other restricted cross-package imports

extracted_rules.title: Use Biome for Code Formatting with Standardized Configuration
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent code formatting across the entire codebase by using Biome with specific formatting rules for line width, indentation, quotes, and semicolons
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All TypeScript/JavaScript files use 110 character line width, 2-space indentation, LF line endings, double quotes for JSX, always include semicolons, use ES5 trailing commas, and always use arrow function parentheses
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code files deviate from the standard formatting rules, such as using single quotes in JSX, omitting semicolons, using different indentation widths, or exceeding line width limits

extracted_rules.title: Default Exports Allowed Only in Next.js Page and Layout Files
extracted_rules.objective: Enforce named exports throughout the codebase for better refactoring and tree-shaking, while allowing default exports only where Next.js requires them (page.tsx and layout.tsx files)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Files use named exports (export const, export function, export class) except for files matching patterns 'apps/web/app/**/page.tsx', 'apps/web/app/**/layout.tsx', and 'apps/web/app/pages/**/*.tsx' which may use default exports
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Non-page/layout files use default exports, or page/layout files fail to export the required default component

extracted_rules.title: Schema and Handler Files Must Be Separated with Type-Safe Patterns
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain separation of concerns and type safety by requiring schema definitions in separate '.schema.ts' files with both Zod schema and TypeScript type exports, while handlers in '.handler.ts' files use these typed schemas
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Schema files export both a TypeScript type (T<Operation>InputSchema) and a corresponding Zod schema (Z<Operation>InputSchema: z.ZodType<T<Operation>InputSchema>), and handler files import and use these typed schemas for validation
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Schema and handler logic are mixed in the same file, schema files lack either TypeScript types or Zod schemas, or handler files perform validation without using the defined schemas

extracted_rules.title: Lint Staged Files Before Commit with Error-on-Warnings Enforcement
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure code quality by running Biome linting on staged files before commits and treating warnings as errors unless explicitly skipped via SKIP_WARNINGS environment variable
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Pre-commit hook runs 'biome lint --error-on-warnings' on staged TypeScript/JavaScript files, 'biome format' on JSON files, and 'prisma format' on schema.prisma, and all checks pass before commit is allowed
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Commits are made with linting warnings or formatting issues, staged files are not checked before commit, or the pre-commit hook is bypassed without proper justification

extracted_rules.title: Environment Variables Must Not Be Accessed Directly in Non-Configuration Code
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent runtime errors and improve testability by avoiding direct process.env access in business logic and instead using centralized configuration modules or environment-specific checks
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Direct process.env access is limited to configuration files, environment detection utilities (isENVProd, isENVDev), and build-time configuration, while business logic receives environment values through dependency injection or configuration objects
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Business logic, handlers, or service classes directly access process.env properties instead of using configuration abstractions or injected values

extracted_rules.title: All Tests Must Use Vitest Framework and UTC Timezone
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent test execution and prevent timezone-related bugs by standardizing on Vitest as the test framework and enforcing UTC timezone for all test runs
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test files use Vitest syntax (vi.mock, vi.fn, describe, it, expect), test commands set TZ=UTC environment variable, and tests do not depend on local timezone settings
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Tests use Jest-specific APIs, test commands omit TZ=UTC setting, or tests fail when run in different timezones

extracted_rules.title: React Components Must Use react-hook-form with Zod Schema Validation
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent form handling and validation by requiring React Hook Form with Zod resolver for all form components, providing type-safe validation and error handling
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Form components use useForm hook with zodResolver, define Zod schemas for form validation, use Controller or register for form fields, and properly handle validation errors with error messages
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Form components implement custom validation logic without react-hook-form, lack Zod schema validation, or fail to properly display validation errors to users

extracted_rules.title: Custom Error Classes Must Use Hierarchical Structure with Typed Codes
extracted_rules.objective: Enable robust error handling and debugging by requiring custom error classes that extend base Error classes with typed error codes, HTTP status codes, and structured error information
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Error classes extend from base error types (HttpError, CalendarAppError, ErrorWithCode), include typed error codes for categorization, provide statusCode for HTTP errors, and include relevant context (URL, method, cause)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code throws generic Error objects, lacks error categorization, omits HTTP status codes for API errors, or fails to include sufficient debugging context

extracted_rules.title: Python Functions Must Include Type Annotations
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure type safety and improve code maintainability by requiring explicit type annotations for all function parameters and return values in Python code, as enforced by basedpyright type checking
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All Python function definitions include type hints for parameters and return types using Python 3.12+ syntax (e.g., list[str] instead of List[str], int | None instead of Optional[int])
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Python function definitions lack type annotations for parameters or return values

extracted_rules.title: Python Code Must Follow Ruff Linting Rules
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent code quality and style by adhering to the project's ruff configuration, which enforces rules for code formatting, import ordering, security checks, and best practices
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All Python code passes ruff format and ruff check --fix without errors, following the rules defined in .ruff.toml including line length of 120 characters, proper import sorting, and security rules (S102, S307, S301, S302, S311)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Python code violates ruff linting rules such as improper formatting, incorrect import ordering, use of print() instead of logging, or security violations like using exec/eval/pickle

extracted_rules.title: Backend Code Must Use Logging Instead of Print Statements
extracted_rules.objective: Enable proper observability and debugging by requiring all output to use the logging module rather than print statements, with logger instances declared at module level
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All logging is performed using logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) declared at module top, with no print() statements in production code (tests are exempt)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains print() statements outside of test files, or logging is performed without proper logger initialization

extracted_rules.title: Python Code Must Use Modern Type Syntax for Python 3.12+
extracted_rules.objective: Leverage modern Python type system features for better code clarity and type safety by using the latest type annotation syntax
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Type annotations use Python 3.12+ syntax: list[T] instead of List[T], dict[K,V] instead of Dict[K,V], int | None instead of Optional[int], and str | int instead of Union[str, int]
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code uses legacy typing imports like List, Dict, Optional, Union from the typing module when modern syntax is available

extracted_rules.title: Python Backend Files Must Not Exceed 800 Lines
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain code readability and modularity by keeping individual Python files under 800 lines, promoting proper code organization and separation of concerns
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All Python files in the backend (api/) contain fewer than 800 lines of code
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Python files in the backend exceed 800 lines and should be split into multiple files

extracted_rules.title: SQLAlchemy Sessions Must Use Context Managers
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure proper database connection management and prevent resource leaks by requiring all SQLAlchemy sessions to be opened with context managers
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All database operations use 'with Session(db.engine, expire_on_commit=False) as session:' pattern for session management
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Database sessions are created without context managers or sessions are not properly closed

extracted_rules.title: Database Queries Must Include tenant_id Scoping
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure data isolation and security in multi-tenant architecture by requiring all database queries to be scoped by tenant_id to prevent cross-tenant data access
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All database queries that access tenant-scoped resources include WHERE clauses filtering by tenant_id
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Database queries access tenant-scoped tables without tenant_id filtering, creating potential data leakage

extracted_rules.title: Python Tests Must Follow pytest AAA Pattern
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain clear and maintainable test structure by requiring all pytest tests to follow the Arrange-Act-Assert pattern for better readability and understanding
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test functions are structured with three distinct sections: Arrange (setup), Act (execution), Assert (verification), with clear separation between phases
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test functions mix setup, execution, and assertion logic without clear separation or organization

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript Must Avoid any Type Annotations
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain type safety in the frontend codebase by avoiding the any type, which bypasses TypeScript's type checking and can lead to runtime errors
extracted_rules.success_criteria: TypeScript code uses specific types or unknown instead of any, with ts/no-explicit-any warnings addressed
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains 'any' type annotations without justified exceptions or proper type definitions

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript Must Use Type Definitions Instead of Interfaces
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistency in type declarations across the codebase by preferring type definitions over interfaces, as enforced by ts/consistent-type-definitions rule
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All TypeScript type declarations use 'type' keyword instead of 'interface' keyword, following the pattern: type MyType = { ... }
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code uses 'interface' declarations instead of 'type' definitions

extracted_rules.title: Frontend User-Facing Strings Must Use i18n Translations
extracted_rules.objective: Enable internationalization and localization by requiring all user-facing text in the frontend to be retrieved from i18n translation files rather than hardcoded
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All user-facing strings are defined in web/i18n/en-US/ translation files and accessed via useTranslation hook with proper namespace options, following dify-i18n/require-ns-option rule
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: User-facing strings are hardcoded in component files instead of using translation keys

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript Files Must Follow Strict TypeScript Configuration
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure type safety and catch potential errors at compile time by enabling strict TypeScript compiler options
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All TypeScript code compiles successfully with strict mode enabled in tsconfig.json, including strict type checking and consistent casing enforcement
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains type errors or inconsistent casing that would fail strict TypeScript compilation

extracted_rules.title: Backend Configuration Must Be Accessed via configs Module
extracted_rules.objective: Centralize configuration management and prevent direct environment variable access by requiring all configuration to be retrieved through the configs module
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Configuration values are accessed through configs.dify_config or related config modules, not via direct os.environ or os.getenv calls
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code directly reads environment variables using os.environ or os.getenv instead of using the configs module

extracted_rules.title: Python Backend Must Use Pydantic v2 for Data Validation
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent data validation and serialization using Pydantic v2 models with proper configuration for DTOs and request/response validation
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All data transfer objects use Pydantic v2 BaseModel with ConfigDict(extra='forbid') by default, and use @field_validator/@model_validator for domain rules
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Data validation uses Pydantic v1 syntax, allows undeclared fields without explicit configuration, or uses custom validation logic instead of Pydantic validators

extracted_rules.title: Backend Errors Must Use Domain-Specific Exceptions
extracted_rules.objective: Provide clear error handling and appropriate HTTP responses by raising domain-specific exceptions from services and translating them to HTTP responses in controllers
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Business logic raises exceptions from services/errors or core/errors modules, and controllers handle these exceptions to return appropriate HTTP responses
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Services return HTTP responses directly, or generic exceptions are raised without domain context

extracted_rules.title: Python Code Must Use Snake Case for Variables and Functions
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent naming conventions across the Python codebase by using snake_case for variables and functions, PascalCase for classes, and UPPER_CASE for constants
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All Python variables and functions use snake_case naming (e.g., user_name, get_user_data), classes use PascalCase (e.g., UserService), and constants use UPPER_CASE (e.g., MAX_RETRIES)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Python code uses camelCase, PascalCase for variables/functions, or inconsistent naming patterns

extracted_rules.title: Frontend ESLint Sonarjs Rules Must Be Followed
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain code quality and prevent common bugs by adhering to SonarJS cognitive complexity and maintainability rules configured in the project
extracted_rules.success_criteria: TypeScript code passes SonarJS linting rules including no-dead-store (error level), max-lines warnings (1000 line limit), and no-variable-usage-before-declaration (error level)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code violates SonarJS rules such as dead stores, files exceeding 1000 lines, or variables used before declaration

extracted_rules.title: Backend Architecture Must Follow Import Layer Constraints
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain clean architecture boundaries by enforcing layer separation through import-linter rules that prevent circular dependencies and upward imports
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Code adheres to import-linter contracts defined in .importlinter, including workflow layer separation (graph_engine → graph → nodes → entities) and domain isolation rules
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Imports violate architectural layers by importing from higher layers or creating circular dependencies not explicitly allowed in .importlinter

extracted_rules.title: Backend Storage Access Must Use Abstraction Layer
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent and secure storage operations by requiring all storage access to go through extensions.ext_storage.storage abstraction
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All file storage operations use extensions.ext_storage.storage interface instead of direct filesystem or cloud storage APIs
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code directly accesses filesystem, S3, Azure Blob, or other storage without using the storage abstraction layer

extracted_rules.title: Backend HTTP Requests Must Use SSRF Proxy Helper
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent Server-Side Request Forgery attacks by requiring all outbound HTTP requests to use the SSRF proxy helper for validation and protection
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All outbound HTTP fetches use core.helper.ssrf_proxy instead of direct httpx, requests, or urllib calls
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code makes outbound HTTP requests without using the SSRF proxy helper, potentially exposing internal resources

extracted_rules.title: Python Code Must Not Override Dunder Methods Unnecessarily
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent subtle bugs and maintain expected Python object behavior by avoiding unnecessary overrides of special methods like __init__, __iadd__, etc.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Special methods (__init__, __iadd__, __str__, __repr__) are only overridden when necessary with proper implementation of relevant special methods documented in coding_style.md
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code overrides dunder methods without clear justification or without implementing complementary methods

extracted_rules.title: Backend Must Avoid Security-Risky Functions
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent remote code execution vulnerabilities by prohibiting the use of dangerous built-in functions that can execute arbitrary code
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Python code does not use exec(), eval(), pickle, marshal, or ast.literal_eval() as enforced by ruff security rules S102, S307, S301, S302
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code uses exec(), eval(), pickle.loads(), marshal.loads(), or ast.literal_eval() which can execute arbitrary code

extracted_rules.title: Python Backend Must Use Deterministic Control Flow
extracted_rules.objective: Optimize for observability and debugging by maintaining deterministic control flow with clear logging and actionable errors
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Code avoids clever hacks, maintains readable control flow, includes tenant/app/workflow identifiers in log context, and logs retryable events at warning level and terminal failures at error level
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code uses obfuscated logic, lacks proper logging context, or mixes logging levels inappropriately

extracted_rules.title: Backend Async Tasks Must Be Idempotent
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure reliability of background processing by requiring all async tasks to be idempotent and log relevant object identifiers for debugging
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All background tasks in tasks/ are idempotent (can be safely retried), log the relevant object identifiers (tenant_id, app_id, etc.), and specify explicit queue selection
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Background tasks are not idempotent, lack proper logging identifiers, or don't specify queue configuration

extracted_rules.title: Frontend Code Must Not Use console Statements
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent debug code from reaching production by treating console statements as warnings that should be removed or replaced with proper logging
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Production frontend code avoids console.log, console.warn, console.error statements as enforced by no-console warning rule
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains console statements that should be removed or replaced with proper logging mechanisms

extracted_rules.title: All Swift Files Must Include Mozilla Public License Header
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure legal compliance and proper licensing by requiring all Swift source files to include the standard Mozilla Public License v2.0 header at the beginning of the file
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Every .swift file begins with the exact Mozilla Public License header comment block: '// This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Swift files are missing the Mozilla Public License header, have an incorrect or modified header text, or have the header in the wrong location (not at the top of the file)

extracted_rules.title: Code Must Pass All Enabled SwiftLint Rules
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent code quality, style, and adherence to Swift best practices by enforcing all SwiftLint rules defined in .swiftlint.yml configuration
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Code passes all SwiftLint rules specified in .swiftlint.yml without warnings or errors, including line length limits (125 characters), closure body length (34 lines), function body length (122 lines), proper spacing, naming conventions, and syntax requirements
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: SwiftLint reports warnings or errors for violations such as exceeding line length, improper spacing, incorrect naming patterns, force unwrapping, force try, legacy API usage, or any other rule violations defined in the configuration

extracted_rules.title: Code Sections Must Be Organized with MARK Comments
extracted_rules.objective: Improve code navigation and readability by using MARK comments to clearly delineate logical sections within Swift files, making it easier for developers to locate specific functionality
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Swift files use '// MARK:' comments to separate distinct code sections such as UI Elements, Properties, Initializers, Public Methods, Private Methods, Protocol Conformance (e.g., '// MARK: - ThemeApplicable'), and other logical groupings
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Swift files lack MARK comments for section organization, or MARK comments are inconsistently applied across similar code structures

extracted_rules.title: UI Elements Must Have Accessibility Identifiers
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure the application is accessible and testable by requiring all interactive UI elements to have unique accessibility identifiers that can be used by UI tests and assistive technologies
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All UI elements (buttons, text fields, views, labels, etc.) that users can interact with or that are tested in UI tests have their accessibilityIdentifier property set, typically using values from ViewModels (e.g., 'viewModel.a11yIdentifier')
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Interactive UI elements are missing accessibilityIdentifier assignments, making them difficult to test or inaccessible to automation tools

extracted_rules.title: UI Components Must Implement ThemeApplicable Protocol
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent theming across the application by requiring all custom UI components to conform to the ThemeApplicable protocol and implement the applyTheme(theme:) method, enabling dynamic theme changes
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Custom UI view classes and components that display visual content conform to the ThemeApplicable protocol and implement the 'func applyTheme(theme: Theme)' method to update colors and visual properties based on the current theme
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: UI components that should support theming do not conform to ThemeApplicable protocol, or implement the protocol but fail to properly update their visual properties in the applyTheme method

extracted_rules.title: Test Classes Must Use XCTest Framework with Proper Structure
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent and reliable test infrastructure by requiring all test classes to inherit from XCTest framework classes and follow standard setup/teardown patterns
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test files import XCTest, test classes inherit from XCTestCase, use setUp() and tearDown() methods for test lifecycle management, test methods start with 'test' prefix, and use XCTAssert family of assertions
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test classes do not inherit from XCTestCase, lack proper setUp/tearDown methods when needed, test methods don't follow naming conventions, or use assertions outside the XCTest framework

extracted_rules.title: UI Components Must Use ViewModel Structs for Configuration
extracted_rules.objective: Promote separation of concerns and testability by requiring UI components to accept configuration through dedicated ViewModel structs rather than individual parameters
extracted_rules.success_criteria: UI components (views, buttons, cells) have corresponding ViewModel structs (e.g., PrimaryRoundedButtonViewModel) that encapsulate all configuration data including titles, identifiers, and styling properties, and components provide a configure(viewModel:) method
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: UI components accept configuration through multiple individual parameters instead of a cohesive ViewModel struct, or ViewModels are missing required properties like a11yIdentifier

extracted_rules.title: System Dependencies Must Be Abstracted Behind Protocols
extracted_rules.objective: Improve testability and maintainability by requiring system dependencies (FileManager, NotificationCenter, etc.) to be abstracted behind protocol interfaces that can be mocked in tests
extracted_rules.success_criteria: System dependencies are accessed through protocol abstractions (e.g., FileManagerProtocol, NotificationProtocol, Application protocol) rather than direct concrete type usage, enabling dependency injection and test mocking
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code directly instantiates or uses concrete system types (FileManager.default, NotificationCenter.default) instead of using protocol abstractions, making the code difficult to test in isolation

extracted_rules.title: UI-Related Code Must Be Annotated with @MainActor
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure thread safety and prevent race conditions by requiring all UI-related code, including view controllers, views, and UI-manipulating methods, to be annotated with @MainActor to guarantee execution on the main thread
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Classes, structs, or methods that interact with UIKit or perform UI updates are marked with @MainActor annotation, ensuring all UI operations execute on the main thread
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: UI-related code lacks @MainActor annotation, potentially allowing UI updates from background threads and causing runtime errors or crashes

extracted_rules.title: Test Files Must Follow Naming Convention
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent test file organization and discoverability by enforcing a standardized naming pattern for test files that clearly indicates their purpose
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Test files are named with 'Tests.swift' or 'Test.swift' suffix (e.g., 'LoggerTests.swift', 'NavigationTest.swift'), clearly identifying them as test files and corresponding to the code they test
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test files do not follow the naming convention, making them difficult to identify as tests or unclear which code they are testing

extracted_rules.title: Properties and Methods Must Have Appropriate Access Control
extracted_rules.objective: Enforce information hiding and prevent unintended external access by requiring explicit access control modifiers (public, internal, private, fileprivate) on all properties and methods based on their intended scope
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All properties and methods have explicit access control modifiers: 'private' for implementation details, 'public' for external API surface, 'internal' for module-level access, ensuring minimal exposure of internal implementation
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Properties or methods lack explicit access control modifiers when they should be private, or are marked public when they are only used internally

extracted_rules.title: Classes That Should Not Be Subclassed Must Be Marked Final
extracted_rules.objective: Improve performance through compiler optimization and prevent unintended inheritance by marking classes as final when they are not designed to be subclassed
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Classes that are not intended for inheritance are marked with the 'final' keyword (e.g., 'final class MenuCell', 'public final class SecondaryRoundedButton'), enabling compiler optimizations and making design intent explicit
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Classes that are not designed for subclassing lack the 'final' keyword, allowing unintended inheritance and missing optimization opportunities

extracted_rules.title: UI Constant Values Must Be Defined in Nested UX Structs
extracted_rules.objective: Organize UI-related constants and magic numbers in a centralized, discoverable location by requiring them to be defined within nested UX structs inside UI component classes
extracted_rules.success_criteria: UI components define layout constants, spacing values, corner radii, insets, and other UI measurements within a private nested struct named 'UX', keeping magic numbers organized and easily modifiable (e.g., 'private struct UX { static let buttonCornerRadius: CGFloat = 12 }')
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: UI constants and magic numbers are scattered throughout the implementation code as literals, or are not organized within a dedicated UX namespace struct

extracted_rules.title: Public APIs Must Have Documentation Comments
extracted_rules.objective: Improve code maintainability and developer experience by requiring all public-facing APIs to include documentation comments that explain their purpose, parameters, and usage
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Public functions, methods, properties, classes, structs, and protocols include triple-slash (///) documentation comments describing their purpose, behavior, parameters, and return values
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Public API elements lack documentation comments, making it difficult for other developers to understand their purpose and proper usage without reading implementation code

extracted_rules.title: UIView Subclasses Must Implement Required Init With FatalError
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent accidental instantiation from Interface Builder and make design decisions explicit by requiring UIView subclasses to implement required init?(coder:) with fatalError when programmatic initialization is intended
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Custom UIView subclasses that are designed for programmatic initialization only implement 'required init?(coder: NSCoder) { fatalError("init(coder:) has not been implemented") }', clearly indicating Interface Builder instantiation is not supported
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: UIView subclasses designed for programmatic use are missing the required init?(coder:) implementation, or implement it without fatalError, potentially allowing unintended Interface Builder instantiation

extracted_rules.title: All Python Source Files Must Import Future Annotations
extracted_rules.objective: Enable forward reference resolution and improve type checking performance by requiring `from __future__ import annotations` at the top of all Python files that use type hints
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Every Python file in src/ that contains type annotations includes `from __future__ import annotations` as the first import statement
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Python files with type annotations lack the `from __future__ import annotations` import statement

extracted_rules.title: Type-Only Imports Must Use TYPE_CHECKING Guards
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent circular import errors and reduce runtime overhead by requiring type-only imports to be placed within TYPE_CHECKING conditional blocks
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All imports used exclusively for type hints are placed within `if TYPE_CHECKING:` blocks, and the imported types are quoted in annotations
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Type-only imports are imported at module level instead of within TYPE_CHECKING guards, or unquoted in annotations

extracted_rules.title: Logger Instances Must Follow Standard Initialization Pattern
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent logging configuration and type safety across the codebase by standardizing logger instantiation
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All logger instances are created using the pattern `logger: "logging.Logger" = get_logger("module_name")` where module_name describes the logger's scope
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Loggers are instantiated using direct logging.getLogger() calls, or lack proper type annotations, or use inconsistent naming patterns

extracted_rules.title: Secret Values Must Use Pydantic Secret Types
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent accidental exposure of sensitive data in logs, error messages, and API responses by requiring proper secret field typing
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All fields containing sensitive data (passwords, tokens, API keys, credentials) are typed as SecretStr, SecretBytes, or SecretDict from Pydantic
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Sensitive credential fields are typed as plain str, bytes, or dict instead of using Pydantic's Secret types

extracted_rules.title: FastAPI Router Endpoints Must Include Docstrings
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure API documentation is automatically generated and endpoints are self-documenting by requiring docstrings on all route handlers
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All functions decorated with @router.get, @router.post, @router.patch, @router.delete, etc. include docstrings describing the endpoint purpose and behavior
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Route handler functions lack docstrings or have only placeholder/empty docstrings

extracted_rules.title: Test Files Must Mirror Source Directory Structure
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain test organization and discoverability by ensuring test files follow the same directory hierarchy as the source code they test
extracted_rules.success_criteria: For every source file at src/prefect/module/file.py, a corresponding test file exists at tests/module/test_file.py with the same relative path structure
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test files are placed in incorrect directories that don't mirror the source structure, or use different naming conventions

extracted_rules.title: Async Functions Must Have Sync Compatibility Wrappers Where Public
extracted_rules.objective: Provide user-friendly API that works in both async and sync contexts by ensuring public async functions offer sync compatibility
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Public async functions that are part of the user-facing API have corresponding sync wrappers using @sync_compatible or explicit sync versions
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: User-facing async functions lack sync compatibility wrappers, forcing users to always use asyncio.run() or similar

extracted_rules.title: Pydantic Models Must Use model_validator for Cross-Field Validation
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure data integrity and proper validation sequencing by using Pydantic's model_validator decorator for validations that depend on multiple fields
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Validation logic that depends on multiple fields uses @model_validator decorator instead of individual field validators
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Cross-field validation is implemented using individual field validators, causing order-dependent validation issues or accessing unvalidated fields

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript Components Must Use Tab Indentation
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent code formatting across the frontend codebase as enforced by Biome configuration
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All TypeScript and TSX files in ui-v2/ use tab characters for indentation as specified in biome.json
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: TypeScript files use spaces for indentation instead of tabs

extracted_rules.title: React Component Test Files Must Be Co-Located
extracted_rules.objective: Improve test discoverability and maintenance by placing test files alongside the components they test
extracted_rules.success_criteria: For every React component file component.tsx, the test file component.test.tsx exists in the same directory
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test files are separated from component files in different directories, making them harder to find and maintain

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript Must Enable Strict Type Checking
extracted_rules.objective: Catch type errors at compile time and ensure type safety throughout the frontend codebase
extracted_rules.success_criteria: TypeScript compiler is configured with strict mode enabled and no-emit flag for validation, as evidenced by validate:types script running tsc -b --noEmit
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: TypeScript strict mode is disabled or type checking is not part of the validation pipeline

extracted_rules.title: Unused Imports Must Be Automatically Removed
extracted_rules.objective: Keep the codebase clean and reduce bundle size by eliminating unused imports via automated linting
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Biome and ESLint configurations enforce noUnusedImports as an error, and pre-commit hooks automatically remove unused imports
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Unused imports remain in the codebase after linting, or the linter is not configured to detect them as errors

extracted_rules.title: Pre-Commit Hooks Must Run Ruff for Python Code Quality
extracted_rules.objective: Enforce code quality standards and catch common issues before code is committed by running Ruff linter and formatter
extracted_rules.success_criteria: The .pre-commit-config.yaml includes ruff-check and ruff-format hooks that run automatically on Python files before commits
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Pre-commit configuration lacks Ruff hooks, or they are configured to not block commits on failures

extracted_rules.title: Database Session Management Must Use Context Managers
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure proper resource cleanup and transaction handling by requiring database sessions to be managed via async context managers
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All database operations use `async with db.session_context(begin_transaction=True) as session:` pattern for session management
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Database sessions are created and managed manually without context managers, or transactions are not properly scoped

extracted_rules.title: API Request Parameters Must Use Pydantic Schema Validation
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure data validation and automatic OpenAPI schema generation by using Pydantic models for all API request/response bodies
extracted_rules.success_criteria: FastAPI route handlers accept request bodies typed as Pydantic schema classes from prefect.server.schemas.actions or prefect.client.schemas
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Route handlers accept raw dictionaries or unvalidated data structures instead of Pydantic models for request bodies

extracted_rules.title: All Source Files Must Include Tri-License Header
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure legal compliance and proper licensing attribution by requiring all C source and header files to include the standard Redis tri-license copyright header (RSALv2/SSPLv1/AGPLv3)
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Every .c and .h file in the src/ directory contains a copyright header with the text 'Licensed under your choice of (a) the Redis Source Available License 2.0 (RSALv2); or (b) the Server Side Public License v1 (SSPLv1); or (c) the GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPLv3)'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Any .c or .h file in src/ lacks the tri-license header or uses an incorrect license format

extracted_rules.title: Use zmalloc/zfree Wrappers Instead of Direct malloc/free
extracted_rules.objective: Enable memory usage tracking and prevent memory leaks by enforcing the use of Redis's zmalloc family of functions (zmalloc, zcalloc, zrealloc, zfree) instead of standard library malloc/free functions
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All dynamic memory allocations in src/ use zmalloc, zcalloc, zrealloc, ztrycalloc, or ztrymalloc functions, and all deallocations use zfree
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Code contains direct calls to malloc(), calloc(), realloc(), or free() outside of the zmalloc implementation itself

extracted_rules.title: Header Files Must Use Double-Underscore Include Guards
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent multiple inclusion errors and maintain consistency by enforcing a standardized naming pattern for include guards in header files
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All .h files use include guards following the pattern #ifndef __FILENAME_H / #define __FILENAME_H / #endif, with double underscores at the start and end
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Header files use include guards with non-standard naming (single underscore, no underscores, or different patterns)

extracted_rules.title: Build Must Compile Without Warnings When -Werror is Enabled
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain high code quality and prevent potentially buggy code from being merged by ensuring the codebase compiles cleanly with compiler warnings treated as errors
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Running 'make REDIS_CFLAGS="-Werror"' completes successfully with no compilation warnings or errors
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Compilation fails or produces warnings when -Werror flag is enabled

extracted_rules.title: Test Functions Must Be Wrapped in REDIS_TEST Preprocessor Blocks
extracted_rules.objective: Separate test code from production code to reduce binary size and prevent test-only code from being included in production builds
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All test functions and test-specific code in source files are enclosed within #ifdef REDIS_TEST / #endif blocks
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Test functions or test-specific code exists in source files without being wrapped in REDIS_TEST conditional compilation blocks

extracted_rules.title: Redis Commands Must Have Corresponding JSON Definition Files
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure all Redis commands are properly documented and structured by maintaining JSON schema definitions in src/commands/ directory for command metadata, arguments, and behavior
extracted_rules.success_criteria: For every command implemented in the codebase, there exists a corresponding .json file in src/commands/ directory with complete command definition including summary, complexity, group, arity, function name, and reply schema
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: A command implementation exists without a corresponding JSON definition file, or the JSON file is incomplete or malformed

extracted_rules.title: Internal Helper Functions Must Be Declared Static
extracted_rules.objective: Enforce proper encapsulation and prevent symbol pollution by ensuring functions that are not part of the public API are declared with static storage class
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Functions that are only used within a single source file are declared with the 'static' keyword
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Helper functions or internal implementation functions are declared without 'static' keyword, making them visible to other compilation units unnecessarily

extracted_rules.title: Generated commands.def Must Be Kept in Sync with Command JSON Files
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent inconsistencies between command definitions and generated code by ensuring the commands.def file is regenerated whenever command JSON files are modified
extracted_rules.success_criteria: After modifying any .json file in src/commands/, running 'make commands.def' produces no git diff in the commands.def file
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Modifications to command JSON files result in a git diff when running 'make commands.def', indicating commands.def is out of sync

extracted_rules.title: All New Source Files Must Include Copyright and SPDX License Headers
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure proper legal attribution and license compliance by requiring standardized copyright and SPDX license identifiers at the top of all source files. This protects the project's legal standing and makes license information machine-readable.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Every new Rust (.rs), TypeScript/JavaScript (.ts, .js), and workflow (.yml) file begins with a copyright header containing 'Copyright 2019-2024 Tauri Programme within The Commons Conservancy' followed by SPDX license identifiers 'SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0' and 'SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT'
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: New source files are missing the copyright header, have incorrect copyright attribution, or lack the required SPDX license identifiers

extracted_rules.title: Rust Code Must Pass Clippy Linting with Warnings Denied
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain high code quality and catch potential bugs, performance issues, and non-idiomatic patterns by enforcing Clippy lints as errors. This ensures all code adheres to Rust best practices and community standards.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All Rust code passes 'cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings' without any warnings or errors
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Running clippy produces any warnings or errors, indicating code that violates Rust best practices or contains potential issues

extracted_rules.title: Rust Code Must Be Formatted According to Project rustfmt Configuration
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent code style across the entire Rust codebase by enforcing automated formatting rules. This improves readability, reduces merge conflicts, and eliminates style debates in code reviews.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All Rust code passes 'cargo fmt --all -- --check' without modifications, adhering to the project's rustfmt.toml configuration (max_width=100, tab_spaces=2, Unix newlines, etc.)
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Running cargo fmt identifies files that require formatting changes, indicating inconsistent code style

extracted_rules.title: TypeScript and JavaScript Code Must Be Formatted with Prettier
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent formatting in frontend and tooling code by enforcing Prettier formatting standards. This ensures uniformity across TypeScript/JavaScript files and prevents style inconsistencies.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All TypeScript and JavaScript files pass prettier format checks without requiring modifications
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Running prettier --check identifies files that need formatting, indicating code that doesn't conform to the project's style

extracted_rules.title: TOML Configuration Files Must Be Formatted with taplo
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure consistent formatting of TOML configuration files (Cargo.toml, rustfmt.toml, etc.) by enforcing taplo formatting standards. This maintains readability and reduces noise in configuration file diffs.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All TOML files pass 'taplo fmt --check --diff' without requiring changes
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Running taplo detects formatting inconsistencies in TOML files

extracted_rules.title: All Source Files Must Follow EditorConfig Standards
extracted_rules.objective: Enforce consistent basic formatting across all file types (charset, indentation, line endings) by adhering to the .editorconfig specification. This ensures cross-editor consistency and prevents common formatting issues.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All files use UTF-8 encoding, 2-space indentation, LF line endings, include a final newline, and have trailing whitespace trimmed
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Files contain CRLF line endings, inconsistent indentation, wrong encoding, missing final newlines, or trailing whitespace

extracted_rules.title: Public APIs Must Include Documentation Comments
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure all public APIs are properly documented to support developers using the framework and to generate comprehensive API documentation. This improves usability and reduces the learning curve for the library.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All public functions, types, modules, and methods include /// documentation comments explaining their purpose, parameters, return values, and usage examples where appropriate
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Public API elements lack documentation comments, or documentation is incomplete/unclear

extracted_rules.title: Error Types Must Use thiserror for Structured Error Handling
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent and ergonomic error handling throughout the codebase by using the thiserror crate for custom error types. This provides clear error messages and proper error propagation.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All custom error enums use #[derive(thiserror::Error)] with appropriate #[error(...)] attributes providing descriptive error messages
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Custom error types implement Display/Error traits manually instead of using thiserror, or error messages are unclear/missing

extracted_rules.title: Public Error Enums Must Be Marked as Non-Exhaustive
extracted_rules.objective: Preserve API flexibility and prevent breaking changes by marking public error enums as #[non_exhaustive]. This allows adding new error variants in the future without breaking downstream code.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Public error enums that may need future variants include the #[non_exhaustive] attribute
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Public error enums that should be extensible lack the #[non_exhaustive] attribute, potentially causing breaking changes when new variants are added

extracted_rules.title: Functions Returning Values Should Be Marked with must_use Where Appropriate
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent logic errors by ensuring return values that represent important state or results are not accidentally ignored. This catches bugs where critical return values are discarded without handling.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Functions returning builders, handles, or results that must be used for correctness include #[must_use] attribute with explanatory messages
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Functions returning important values that should not be ignored lack #[must_use], allowing silent errors

extracted_rules.title: Platform-Specific Code Must Use Appropriate Conditional Compilation
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure code compiles correctly on all target platforms by properly gating platform-specific functionality behind #[cfg(...)] attributes. This prevents compilation failures and enables cross-platform builds.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Platform-specific code uses #[cfg(target_os = "...")] or similar attributes to conditionally compile only on supported platforms, and includes appropriate #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(...)))] for documentation
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Platform-specific APIs are used without conditional compilation guards, causing build failures on unsupported platforms

extracted_rules.title: Tests Must Be Organized in cfg(test) Modules Named 'tests'
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent test organization and ensure tests are only compiled during testing by placing unit tests in properly configured modules. This keeps production binary sizes small and test code clearly separated.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Unit tests are placed in 'mod tests' blocks with #[cfg(test)] attribute at the module level
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Tests are not properly gated with #[cfg(test)] or use inconsistent module naming, causing tests to compile in production builds

extracted_rules.title: Changes Requiring a Version Bump Must Include a Change File
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure proper version management and changelog generation by requiring change files for all changes that will be released. This provides clear release documentation and enables automated versioning.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Pull requests that modify functionality include a markdown file in the .changes directory following the covector format, specifying the appropriate version bump (major/minor/patch) and change summary
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: PRs with user-facing changes lack a corresponding change file, preventing proper version tracking and changelog generation

extracted_rules.title: Generated Files Must Be Kept in Sync with Source Code
extracted_rules.objective: Prevent runtime errors and inconsistencies by ensuring generated files (TypeScript API bundles, JSON schemas) are regenerated when their source code changes. This maintains integrity between source and generated artifacts.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: When source files for generated artifacts change (packages/api/src/**, crates/tauri-utils/src/config.rs, schema files), the corresponding generated files are updated by running the appropriate build commands
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Generated files become stale and out of sync with their source code, causing type mismatches, missing features, or validation errors

extracted_rules.title: Unsafe Code Must Include Safety Documentation
extracted_rules.objective: Ensure unsafe code blocks are properly justified and their safety invariants are clearly documented to prevent undefined behavior. This makes code review more effective and helps maintainers understand safety requirements.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: All unsafe blocks and functions include comments explaining why the code is safe, what invariants must be maintained, and under what conditions the code is valid
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Unsafe code lacks documentation of safety invariants, making it impossible to verify correctness or maintain the code safely

extracted_rules.title: Functions Should Return Result Types for Fallible Operations
extracted_rules.objective: Enable proper error handling and propagation by using Result<T, E> return types for operations that can fail. This makes error conditions explicit in the API and allows callers to handle errors appropriately.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Operations that can fail (I/O, parsing, validation, etc.) return Result<T> with appropriate error types rather than panicking or returning Option
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Fallible operations use panic!, unwrap(), expect(), or Option where Result would be more appropriate, hiding error conditions from callers

extracted_rules.title: Workspace Members Must Use Consistent Metadata
extracted_rules.objective: Maintain consistent package metadata across all workspace members by inheriting common fields from workspace.package in Cargo.toml. This ensures uniform author attribution, licensing, repository links, and Rust edition.
extracted_rules.success_criteria: Individual crate Cargo.toml files inherit workspace-level metadata (authors, license, repository, homepage, edition, rust-version) rather than duplicating them
extracted_rules.failure_criteria: Crates duplicate metadata that should be inherited from the workspace, causing inconsistencies in licensing, authorship, or other package information
